name: Coleman, Kellee

evidence: Kellee Coleman is a City of Austin Equity Office staffer, matching the filing's 'COA' employer string, 'Manager' occupation, and Austin location; she is listed by name and department on the AFSCME Local 1624 executive board roster as a City Vice President — Local 1624 represents City of Austin and Travis County employees. Zip 78748 could not be independently confirmed and more than one Kellee Coleman appears in Austin-area search results, so identity rests on name + City of Austin employment rather than address. A secondary claim of Dell Medical School Community Strategy Team membership surfaced only via a LinkedIn profile that could not be read directly (HTTP 999), so it is not recorded as an affiliation. No FEC contribution record (pipeline count 0), no TEC lobbyist registration, and no pro-Israel, pro-Palestine, oil & gas, gun-rights, gun-control, or defense-contractor ties found.

affiliations.snippet: Executive board roster lists under City Vice Presidents: 'Kellee Coleman (City of Austin - Equity Office).' Local 1624 is the union representing City of Austin and Travis County employees.

name: Duffy, Joseph
resolved_employer: JMD Public Strategies (founder/principal, Chicago government-relations and campaign consulting firm); concurrently Executive Director, Climate Jobs Illinois
evidence: Joe Duffy is founder and principal of JMD Public Strategies, a Chicago-based government relations and campaign consulting firm — an exact match to the filing's employer string, Chicago 60657 location, and 'Consultant' occupation. Career Democratic operative: campaign manager for Kwame Raoul's 2018 Illinois AG race, executive director of the Missouri Democratic Party, Obama 2008 / Organizing for America, and Iowa Democratic Party coordinated campaign director (2014). His out-of-state donation to Amber Goodwin is consistent with a gun-violence-prevention professional network — Goodwin founded a gun violence prevention organization and Duffy ran Everytown's Nevada background-check ballot initiative. No TEC lobbyist registration (he is an Illinois practitioner); no pro-Israel, pro-Palestine, oil & gas, gun-rights, or defense-contractor ties found.

name: Golden, Nicole
resolved_employer: Texas Gun Sense — Executive Director
evidence: Nicole Golden, MSSW, is Executive Director of Texas Gun Sense, the Austin-headquartered statewide gun-violence-prevention nonprofit; she joined as Development Manager in 2021 and became ED in spring 2022. The filing's employer ('Texas Gun Sense') and occupation ('Executive Director') match exactly, and Texas Legislature witness lists document her at the Capitol as 'Executive director (Texas Gun Sense), Austin, TX.' She co-founded the Austin group of Moms Demand Action after Sandy Hook. UNRESOLVED THREAD: a search-engine index of the Texas Ethics Commission's 2023 and 2025 Registered Lobbyists PDFs indicates a 'Nicole G. Golden (Ms.)' entry, which would be plausible for a nonprofit ED who testifies at the Capitol, but the TEC PDFs are image/compressed and could not be read first-hand, and no client list or identity match could be confirmed — so a lobbyist registration is NOT recorded as an affiliation here and warrants a manual check at https://www.ethics.state.tx.us/data/search/lobby/2025/2025RegisteredLobbyists.pdf. No pro-Israel, pro-Palestine, oil & gas, gun-rights, or defense-contractor ties found.

affiliations.snippet: Texas Senate committee witness list, 89th Legislature: Nicole Golden registered AGAINST SB 706, identified as 'Executive director (Texas Gun Sense), Austin, TX.'

affiliations.org: Moms Demand Action for Gun Sense in America (Austin group)
affiliations.snippet: Golden co-founded the Austin chapter of Moms Demand Action following the Sandy Hook shooting, and specializes in public speaking, advocacy, and coalition-building.
